PM Holness:
nitter.net/andrewholnessjm/status…

Andrew Holness (@AndrewHolnessJM)

Sometimes when we speak about the assistance Jamaica has received from our international partners, it can be difficult for the public to fully visualize what that support looks like on the ground. This is especially true when we refer to innovations such as the medical cube provided by India.

The medical cube is a fully integrated, rapidly deployable unit designed to strengthen emergency medical response in the aftermath of disasters. It is equipped with essential medical supplies and pharmaceuticals, allowing frontline responders to deliver urgent care quickly and efficiently in the field when time is critical.

This support from India will significantly enhance Jamaica’s disaster response capacity, strengthening our ability to respond not only to major events such as Hurricane Melissa, but also to other emergencies that may arise in the future.

This gesture reflects more than friendship between our two countries. It demonstrates shared innovation, solidarity, and a commitment to building resilience in the face of increasingly frequent and severe natural disasters.

I wish to express sincere gratitude to Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Dr. S. Jaishankar, and the people of India for this meaningful contribution to Jamaica’s disaster preparedness and national resilience.

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: 01:05 AM IST

The Indian Air Force (IAF) launches high-precision strikes from within Indian airspace. Group Captain Kunal Kalra, piloting a Sukhoi-30MKI, fires the first missile into a terrorist hideout. Rafale jets armed with SCALP long-range cruise missiles and Hammer bombs were utilized to penetrate Pakistani air defenses.

The IAF utilized "stand-off" precision weaponry, meaning the jets released their payloads from well within Indian airspace. This allowed India to hit targets up to 300km away (like Bahawalpur) without crossing the international border or the Line of Control (LoC), significantly reducing the risk to pilots from Pakistani Surface-to-Air Missiles (SAMs).

Weaponry & Aircraft Roles

1⃣Sukhoi-30MKI (Group Capt. Kalra's Unit): These jets served as the multi-role backbone. They used BrahMos-NG (Next Generation) and Crystal Maze missiles to take out fortified command-and-control centers.

2⃣Rafale Jets: Armed with SCALP cruise missiles, they were the "surgical" arm. SCALPs are designed to fly low to evade radar and penetrate deep into concrete bunkers (like the underground facilities at Muridke) before exploding.

3⃣Hammer Bombs: These were used for "shorter" long-range strikes against moving targets or smaller hideouts, offering high maneuverability even in the mountainous terrain of PoJK.

4⃣Mirage-2000s: Provided electronic warfare support, jamming Pakistani radars to create a "blind window" for the Sukhois and Rafales to operate.
